signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: RBNZ 2.5%. RBNZ's Conway warns oil-driven inflation is persistent, fueling rate hike bets. Crowded spec shorts face significant unwind risk if the RBNZ delivers a hawkish surprise; NZD/USD at 0.5758 stalls previous declines as dip-buyers emerge. Real rate at -0.58% remains accommodative despite hiking cycle.

signals.title: USD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.8147 driven by a 1.115% G10 leader-to-laggard spread. USD outperformance (avg +0.338%) vs CHF weakness (avg -0.777%) is supported by a +375bp Fed-SNB carry advantage. Despite normal volatility (VIX 17.2), the options market prices an extreme +13.9% VRP, suggesting heavy hedging for a CHF reversal that has yet to materialize in spot. USD CESI (+65) confirms sustained data outperformance vs CHF neutral data.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Options Skew + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.44 holding above the critical 160.00 intervention threshold. +275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid, but 25d RR at -1.58 (Strong Put Skew) signals institutional demand for downside protection against BoJ action. JPY COT LF net short -104,231 (29.1% long) remains crowded; any intervention or hawkish shift at the July 31 BoJ meeting risks a violent carry unwind. VIX at 17.2 keeps carry dominant for now.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + BoC MPR
signals.text: USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation at -0.885 vs +0.049 norm (z=-2.05σ) marks a significant regime break; CAD is failing to track the 0.92% intraday rise in WTI. Market focus shifts to today's BoC Monetary Policy Report and Governor Macklem's press conference. CAD COT LF net short -87,846 (22.6% long) reflects heavy bearish sentiment ahead of the 15 Jul meeting. Watch for BoC growth revisions to confirm or invalidate the CAD laggard status.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + CESI Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1386 shows a +2.3% VRP (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.8%), indicating the options market is pricing in US CPI event risk. EUR CESI (-12) continues to signal persistent data misses relative to the USD CESI (+65) strength. While spot remains bid near 1.1385, the +150bp USD carry advantage and negative data surprises for the Eurozone limit upside potential ahead of the July 23 ECB meeting.
